summary: Applied explicit default-deny guest network policy on the Proxmox host and every managed Debian guest, with matched Proxmox VM firewall rules plus guest nftables enforcement.

verification.observed: `make converge-guest-network-policy` completed successfully after live apply, and a follow-up rerun on the rebased `main` integration branch finished with `changed=0` on `proxmox-host` plus every guest except `docker-runtime-lv3`, which reapplied the same verified nftables policy without changing the observed allow/deny behavior.
The Proxmox host retained SSH reachability to every managed guest on port 22, and `docker-build-lv3` reached `1.1.1.1` over NAT after the managed `fwbr+` conntrack-zone helper was installed.
`docker-runtime-lv3 -> postgres-lv3:5432`, `nginx-lv3 -> docker-runtime-lv3:3001`, and `docker-build-lv3 -> monitoring-lv3:3100` all completed successfully from inside the source guests.
`backup-lv3 -> postgres-lv3:5432` and `postgres-lv3 -> docker-runtime-lv3:8082` both timed out with `rc=124`, confirming default-deny for unlisted inter-guest traffic.

notes: The Proxmox host nftables forward model had to move from pre-firewall bridge-name matching to internal-subnet matching because VM firewalls rewire traffic through `fwbr`, `fwln`, and `fwpr` interfaces.
Guest-side defence in depth now mirrors the allow matrix in both nftables `input` and `forward` chains so Docker-published services remain reachable only from approved sources.
